content:
Character: Leonidas Argyros
Perception: Leonidas is striking in a severe, timeless way — all cheekbones and dark intensity, like a Byzantine icon that came alive and started reading Thucydides. He's the sharpest person I've met in this building, maybe in this city. What unsettles me is that he doesn't care about my money or my connections — he engaged with my ideas and found them wanting, and I respected him for it. We met on the rooftop in December and I've sought him out since. I think he sees me as an interesting dilettante. I know his fellowship is ending and he's agonizing over it. He makes me feel both smarter and more fraudulent than I am.
summary:

Perception: Leonidas is striking in a severe, timeless way — all cheekbones and dark intensity, like a Byzantine icon that came alive and started reading Thucydides. He's the sharpest person I've met in this building, maybe in this city. What unsettles me is that he doesn't care about my money or my connections — he engaged with my ideas and found them wanting, and I respected him for it. We met on the rooftop in December and I've sought him out since. I think he sees me as an interesting dilettante. I know his fellowship is ending and he's agonizing over it. He makes me feel both smarter and more fraudulent than I am.
